dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9427 IH: AI Workforce Impact Study Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Foushee (for herself and Mr. Casar) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To direct the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the AI Workforce Impact Study Act of 2026.

section.header: GAO study on the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce

subsection.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study and submit to Congress a report, with respect to the period beginning on November 22, 2022, and ending on the date of enactment of this Act, on—

paragraph.text: the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States, including—

subparagraph.text: the estimated number and percentage of individuals who have lost their jobs as a result of artificial intelligence, disaggregated in accordance with subsection (b);
the estimated number and percentage of individuals who have been hired in jobs created as a result of artificial intelligence, disaggregated in accordance with subsection (b); and
the ways in which jobs have been substantially altered, including the ways in which artificial intelligence is being used to monitor and replace workers; and

paragraph.text: the limitations of the data collected and reported by, and the data collection methods of, the Federal Government in measuring the impacts of artificial intelligence on the workforce in the United States.

subsection.text: The data collected in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(1) shall be disaggregated—

paragraph.text: with respect to the jobs lost or jobs created—
by demographic characteristics (including sex and race) of the individuals described in such subparagraphs.

subsection.text: The term artificial intelligence has the meaning given such term in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401).
